title: Ducted pupil expansion
abstract: A device including a waveguide having a first waveguide surface and a second waveguide surface parallel to the first waveguide surface is disclosed. The device may include a first light coupling device operatively coupled to the waveguide. The first light coupling device may include a first duct structure and a second duct structure oriented to reflect in-coupled light. Each of the first duct structure and the second duct structure may includes a first planar region and a second planar region parallel to the first planar region and a first surface and a second surface parallel to the first surface. The device may also include a second light coupling device disposed between the first waveguide surface and the second waveguide surface. The second light coupling device may be to positioned to receive reflected in- coupled light from the first light coupling device.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS What is claimed is: 1. A device comprising: a waveguide having a first waveguide surface and a second waveguide surface parallel to the first waveguide surface; a first light coupling device operatively coupled to the waveguide, wherein the first light coupling device includes a first duct structure and a second duct structure, each of the first and second duct structures being oriented to reflect in-coupled light and comprising: a first planar region and a second planar region parallel to the first planar region; and a first surface and a second surface parallel to the first surface, wherein the first and second surfaces are orthogonal to the first and second planar regions; and a second light coupling device disposed between the first waveguide surface and the second waveguide surface, wherein the second light coupling device is positioned to receive reflected in-coupled light from the first light coupling device. 2. The device of claim 1, wherein the first duct structure and the second duct structure are oriented to reflect rays of in-coupled light incident on the corresponding first planar region as reflected light having a first angle of reflection with respect to a first axis corresponding to a propagation direction of a respective duct structure and a second angle of reflection with respect to a second axis corresponding to a transverse dimension of the propagation direction. 3. The device of claim 1, wherein at least one of the first duct structure or the second duct structure of the first light coupling device further comprises: one or more reflective coating elements disposed at the first planar region of the respective first or second duct structure; and one or more reflective coating elements disposed at the second planar region of the respective first or second duct structure. 4. The device of claim 3, wherein at least one of the one or more reflective coating elements is configured to sustain partial light reflectivity of light incident 

 on a respective first or second planar region for one or more angles of reflection spanning at least some angles below a corresponding critical angle of the first or second planar region. 5. The device of claim 1, wherein at least one of the first duct structure or the second duct structure of the first light coupling device further comprises: one or more reflective coating elements disposed at the first surface of the respective first or second duct structure; and one or more reflective coating elements disposed at the second surface of the respective first or second duct structure. 6. The device of claim 5, wherein at least one of the one or more reflective coating elements is configured to sustain spatial uniformity of reflectivity corresponding to incident light for at least one of an angle of incidence of the incident light, a wavelength of the incident light, or a polarization of the incident light. 7. The device of claim 5, wherein at least one of the one or more reflective coating elements is configured to spatially vary reflectivity corresponding to incident light based at least in part on an angle of incidence of the incident light. 8. The device of claim 5, wherein each of the one or more reflective coating elements comprises at least one of a metallic coating element, a dielectric coating element, or a polymer film. 9. The device of claim 1, wherein the first light coupling device further comprises a light input component and each of the first and second duct structures is angularly offset from a longitudinal axis of the light input component. 10. The device of claim 9, wherein the light input component comprises at least one of a beveled edge component, a coupling prism, or a prism plinth. 11. The device of claim 1, wherein the second light coupling device further comprises: a volume holographic light coupling device structured to reflect at least a portion of incident light as reflected light, the incident light having a first angle of incidence within a total internal reflection (TIR) range with respect a first axis corresponding to a surface normal of the waveguide and a second angle of incidence with respect to a second 

 axis different from the first axis, and the reflected light having a first angle of reflection within the TIR range and a second angle of reflection with respect to the second axis, the second angle of reflection being different from the second angle of incidence. 12. The device of claim 1, wherein at least one of the first duct structure or the second duct structure of the first light coupling device further comprises: a first end contiguous to the first and second planar regions and the first and second surfaces of the respective first or second duct structure; and a second end contiguous to the first and second planar regions and the first and second surfaces of the respective first or second duct structure. 13. The device of claim 12, wherein the second end is offset from the first end by a spatial length substantially equal to a length of the respective first or second duct structure in the propagation direction. 14. The device of claim 12, wherein at least one of the first end or the second end comprises an end reflective coating element. 15. The device of claim 14, wherein the end reflective coating element comprises an absorptive coating element or a fully reflective coating element. 16. The device of claim 1, wherein the first duct structure is oriented to reflect in-coupled light corresponding to a first range of vertical field angles and the second duct structure is oriented to reflect in-coupled light corresponding to a second range of vertical field angles, the second range of vertical field angles including at least some vertical field angles different from vertical field angles of the first range of vertical field angles. 17. The device of claim 1, wherein at least one of the first duct structure or the second duct structure of the first light coupling device further comprises: a fully reflective coating element disposed at the first planar region of the respective first or second duct structure. 18. The device of claim 1, wherein at least one of the first duct structure or the second duct structure of the first light coupling device further comprises: a fully reflective coating element disposed at the first planar region of the respective first or second duct structure; 

 a fully reflective coating element disposed at the second planar region of the respective first or second duct structure; and a partially reflective coating element disposed at the first surface of the respective first or second duct structure. 19. The device of claim 18, wherein the first surface of the respective duct structure is a surface adhered to the first waveguide surface of the waveguide. 20. The device of claim 1, wherein the first light coupling device further comprises: a media layer block, the media layer block comprising an absorptive coating element or a partially reflective coating element. 21. The device of claim 1, wherein the first planar region of the first duct structure is orthogonally aligned with the first planar region of the second duct structure. 22. The device of claim 1, wherein the first duct structure includes a first bevel planar region and the second duct structure includes a second bevel planar region. 23. The device of claim 22, wherein the first bevel planar region of the first duct structure is oriented parallel to the second bevel planar region of the second duct structure. 24. The device of claim 1, wherein the first duct structure and the second duct structure are joined to a reference flat with an optical adhesive element. 25. The device of claim 1, wherein the second light coupling device includes a first shadow casting element and a second shadow casting element. 26. The device of claim 25, wherein the first shadow casting element and the second shadow casting element are located at a partially reflective coating element. 27. The device of claim 25, wherein the first shadow casting element is located at the first waveguide surface and the second shadow casting element is located at the second waveguide surface. 28. The device of claim 1, wherein the second light coupling device has a first reflective axis that is oriented on a plane parallel to the first waveguide surface. 29. The device of claim 1, wherein the second light coupling device comprises: a grating medium; and a first grating structure within the grating medium, the first grating structure being structured to reflect at least a portion of incident light of a first wavelength about a first reflective axis at a first angle of incidence within the TIR range with respect the first reflective axis. 30. The device of claim 29, wherein the first grating structure is further configured to reflect at least a portion of incident light of a wavelength about the first reflective axis at a first range of incidence angles including a first incidence angle, each incidence angle of the first range of incidence angles being within the TIR range with respect the first reflective axis. 31. The device of claim 29, wherein the first grating structure is further configured to reflect light of a wavelength about the first reflective axis offset from a surface normal of the grating medium at a first range of incidence angles including a first incidence angle, and wherein each incidence angle of the first range of incidence angles is greater than a second incidence angle. 32. The device of claim 31, wherein the wavelength comprises one of: a visible red light wavelength, a visible blue light wavelength, or a visible green light wavelength. 33. The device of claim 29, wherein the first grating structure comprises at least three volume holograms, each volume hologram of the at least three volume holograms corresponding to a unique incidence angle within the first range of incidence angles, and wherein an adjacent ÎÎÎÎ¿Î for the at least three holograms has a mean value that resides between 1.0 x 10 4 and 1.0 x 10 6 radians per meter (rad/m). 34. The device of claim 29, wherein the second light coupling device further comprises: a second grating structure within the grating medium, the second grating structure being structured to reflect at least a portion of incident light of the first wavelength 

 about another reflective axis at another angle of incidence different from a first incidence angle. 35. The device of claim 34, wherein the other reflective axis of the second grating structure has a reflective axis angle that differs from a reflective axis angle of the first reflective axis by 0.25 degrees or less. 36. The device of claim 1, wherein the second light coupling device comprises a grating medium that is at least 70 Î¼Î¹Î· thick and the grating medium comprises a plurality of volume holograms structured to Bragg-match incident light for at least some angles of incidence within the TIR range. 37. A device comprising: a waveguide having a first waveguide surface, a second waveguide surface parallel to the first waveguide surface, and an planar region portion aligned on a plane different from a plane of the first waveguide surface; a first light coupling device operatively coupled to the waveguide, wherein the first light coupling device includes at least one duct structure, the at least one duct structure comprising: a first planar region and a second planar region parallel to the first planar region; and a first surface and a second surface parallel to the first surface, wherein the first and second surfaces are orthogonal to the first and second planar regions; a light input portion positioned on the first light coupling device; and a second light coupling device disposed between the first waveguide surface and the second waveguide surface, wherein the second light coupling device comprises a volume holographic light coupling device. 38. The device of claim 37, wherein the second light coupling device has a reflective axis that is oriented on a plane orthogonal to the plane of the first waveguide surface. 39. The device of claim 37, wherein the light input portion comprises an aperture. 40. The device of claim 39, wherein the aperture is oriented on a substrate section of the first light coupling device, the substrate section corresponding to at least one of an end of the at least one duct structure. 41. The device of claim 39, wherein the aperture is oriented on a substrate section of the first light coupling device, the substrate section corresponding to at least one of a corner of the at least one duct structure. 42. The device of claim 37, further comprising: an image-bearing light source; and an optical element for receiving image bearing light as in-coupled light and projecting the in-coupled light to the light input portion. 43. The device of claim 37, wherein the second light coupling device has a spatially- varying reflective axis that is oriented on a plane orthogonal to a plane of the first waveguide surface. 44. The device of claim 37, wherein the light input portion comprises an elongated surface section having a longitudinal center substantially aligned with a longitudinal center of an eyebox associated with the second light coupling device.
